title: JavaScript Runtime, Tooling, and Evidence · Independent Script Trial
summary: Trace a small service-status script from source through parsing, execution, console evidence, and a user-visible result. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a reproducible service-status runtime notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish ECMAScript language behavior from browser, worker, and server host APIs.
Trace source parsing, module instantiation, execution contexts, jobs, and observable output.
Use console evidence, breakpoints, scope inspection, watch expressions, and stack traces without relying on logging alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
